Title: Control of fruit softening and Ascorbic acid accumulation by manipulation of SlIMP3 in tomato

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases AsA accumulation but does not increase cell wall thickness, fruit firmness, or delay fruit softening.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of SlDHAR in Ailsa Craig tomato markedly increased total and reduced ascorbate in leaves, stems, and fruits. 2. Despite higher AsA, SlDHAR overexpression did not alter fruit water loss, firmness, cell wall thickness, or softening. 3. The results indicate that elevated AsA alone is insufficient to delay tomato fruit softening in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fruit-specific/transgenic overexpression under CaMV 35S; qRT-PCR; ascorbate quantification; storage assay; firmness measurement; TEM cell wall observation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: dehydroascorbate reductase; glutathione-dependent AsA recycling enzyme
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable benefit for fruit softening or shelf-life despite increased AsA; no reported effect on development.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value for extending tomato shelf-life when used alone; useful as a control showing that boosting AsA recycling is insufficient to improve firmness.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparison gene experimentally tested in Results to distinguish AsA increase from myoinositol/cell wall effects.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: myo-inositol monophosphatase 3 / L-galactose-1-phosphate phosphatase-like bifunctional enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis and myo-inositol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: L-galactose pathway of AsA biosynthesis; myo-inositol monophosphate dephosphorylation step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream in AsA pathway; terminal dephosphorylation step in myo-inositol formation from inositol monophosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AsA functions as antioxidant and nutritional metabolite; myoinositol supports cell wall biogenesis and improves postharvest firmness/shelf-life
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit, especially developing/ripening fruit; also leaf and stem for AsA measurements
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of AsA and myoinositol, increases cell wall thickness and cell wall sugars, delays fruit softening, decreases water loss, extends shelf-life, and enhances Botrytis cinerea tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlIMP3 is highly expressed in tomato fruit, especially immature green fruit, and encodes a bifunctional phosphatase with activity toward L-Gal 1-P and D-Ins 3-P. 2. Overexpression increased total and reduced AsA in multiple tissues and increased fruit myoinositol, uronic acid, rhamnose, xylose, mannose, and galactose. 3. Overexpression increased fruit cell wall thickness, firmness, shelf-life, and resistance to Botrytis cinerea, while antisense suppression reduced AsA and softened fruit. 4. Similar firmness and cell wall phenotypes were reproduced in both Micro-Tom and Ailsa Craig backgrounds. 5. Comparison with SlDHAR showed that the softening phenotype is linked to myoinositol/cell wall biogenesis rather than AsA increase alone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein purification and enzyme kinetics; substrate assays with L-Gal 1-P and D-Ins 3-P; LiCl inhibition assay; transgenic overexpression and antisense lines; qRT-PCR; GUS promoter assay; metabolite profiling; HPLC; GC-MS; TEM; fruit storage/firmness assay; pathogen infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IMP/VTC4-like inositol monophosphatase family with conserved DPLDGT, WDXAAG, and GEET motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates L-Gal 1-P to L-galactose in the ascorbate pathway and dephosphorylates D-Ins 3-P to myoinositol in myoinositol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pathway intermediate for AsA and directly forms myoinositol that contributes to cell wall precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a critical control point for AsA accumulation and myoinositol-dependent cell wall biogenesis in tomato fruit, but not formally established as the sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ubiquitous, highest in fruit; strong in immature green fruit and reduced during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ubiquitous in roots, shoots, leaves, flowers, and fruits; highest in fruits, especially immature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; native SlIMP3 promoter used for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: postharvest storage at 25°C; Botrytis cinerea inoculation; exogenous myoinositol comparison
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable penalty on fruit yield, fruit weight, development, maturation, or ethylene production was reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or metabolic engineering to improve tomato AsA content, fruit firmness, shelf-life, and postharvest pathogen tolerance; may be useful alone or stacked with other shelf-life genes; minimal reported growth penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downregulation decreased AsA but did not decrease fruit myoinositol, suggesting redundancy from other IMP isoforms such as SlIMP2; LiCl inhibited activity; apparent Km was 0.29 mM for L-Gal 1-P and 0.28 mM for D-Ins 3-P.
